pairs.question: Which teams won the 2013 American League Division Series in 2013 and what were the series results?
pairs.answer: The Boston Red Sox (East Division champions) defeated the Tampa Bay Rays (Wild Card Game winner) 3–1 in their series. The Detroit Tigers (Central Division champions) defeated the Oakland Athletics (West Division champions) 3–2 in their series.

pairs.question: How many times had the Tampa Bay Rays and Boston Red Sox faced each other in the postseason prior to the 2013 ALDS?
pairs.answer: The 2013 ALDS was the second time the Tampa Bay Rays and Boston Red Sox faced each other in the postseason. Their first postseason meeting was the 2008 American League Championship Series (ALCS), which the Rays won 4 games to 3.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of the 2013 World Series, and which team won?
pairs.answer: The Boston Red Sox won the 2013 World Series after defeating the National League champion St. Louis Cardinals.

pairs.question: How many total postseason matchups have there been between the Detroit Tigers and the Oakland Athletics as of 2013?
pairs.answer: By 2013, the Detroit Tigers and Oakland Athletics had faced each other in the postseason four times.

pairs.question: Provide a list of all the postseason matchups between the Detroit Tigers and Oakland Athletics prior to 2013, including the years and winners.
pairs.answer: The Detroit Tigers and Oakland Athletics faced each other in the postseason three times before 2013: 1) 1972 ALCS (Oakland Athletics won 3–2), 2) 2006 ALCS (Detroit Tigers won 4–0), and 3) 2012 ALDS (Detroit Tigers won 3–2).

pairs.question: Which television networks broadcasted the 2013 American League Division Series games?
pairs.answer: Most of the 2013 American League Division Series games were carried by TBS, with some games broadcast on MLB Network.

pairs.question: What is the structure of the American League Division Series (ALDS) in Major League Baseball's postseason?
pairs.answer: The ALDS consists of two best-of-five-game series played to determine which teams advance to the American League Championship Series (ALCS). The three divisional winners (seeded 1-3 based on regular-season record) and a fourth team — the winner of a one-game Wild Card playoff — participate in the ALDS.
